s2_papers.title: CAMP: Towards Certified Robustness for Language Models via Randomized Smoothing
s2_papers.abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed in safety-critical applications. We propose CAMP, a framework for certifying the robustness of text classifiers under word substitution attacks using randomized smoothing. Unlike prior work that focuses on image models, CAMP extends certification guarantees to discrete text inputs by modeling word substitution as a probabilistic noise process. Experiments on GLUE benchmarks demonstrate certified accuracy of 72% under attack radius r=1, outperforming all existing baselines. Future work includes extending these guarantees to generative models and open-ended generation tasks.
s2_papers.tldr: CAMP certifies NLP model robustness under word-substitution attacks using randomized smoothing, achieving state-of-the-art certified accuracy on GLUE benchmarks.

s2_papers.title: Empowering Molecule Discovery for Antibiotic Development with a Few-Shot Learning Based Approach
s2_papers.abstract: Antibiotic resistance is one of the most pressing challenges in global health. We present a few-shot learning approach to identify candidate molecules with antibiotic properties. Our model leverages contrastive pretraining on molecular graphs and achieves strong generalization from as few as 10 labeled examples. We validate on three datasets including a newly curated Gram-negative bacteria benchmark. Future work: extending to multi-target resistance prediction and integrating structural protein data for binding affinity estimation.
s2_papers.tldr: A few-shot learning framework for antibiotic drug discovery that generalizes from very limited labeled molecular data.
